---
title: "Gen3: Public BTS Replication"
subtitle: "Nationwide Public Data | 19,150,000 flights | Reproducible Results"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-location: right
    toc-depth: 2
    number-sections: false
    page-layout: full
    smooth-scroll: true
---

```{=html}
<nav style="margin-bottom:1.5rem;">
  <small><a href="index.html">Home</a> › <a href="research-overview-en.html">Research Overview</a> › Gen3</small>
  <br>
  <a href="research-overview-en.html" style="display:inline-block;margin-top:0.5rem;padding:0.3rem 0.8rem;border:1px solid #ccc;border-radius:4px;text-decoration:none;font-size:0.9rem;">← Back to Research Overview</a>
</nav>
```

::: {.panel-tabset}

## Overview

**Motivation:** Gen2 used AA's proprietary Snowflake data, which cannot be publicly reproduced. Validation on public data was needed.

**Data Source:** BTS (Bureau of Transportation Statistics) public data

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
**Scale:** 19,150,000 U.S. domestic flights
:::

**Goal:** Replicate and extend Gen2's methodological framework on public data

**Key Differences:**

- Gen2: Single airline (AA), single hub (DFW)
- Gen3: All U.S. carriers, all flights

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
**AUC 0.7681** (Chimera model)
:::

## SHAP Source-Group Analysis {#shap}

**Method:** Group all features by data source and compute total SHAP contribution per group.

**Results:**

| Source Group | SHAP Contribution |
|---|---|
| Tail Chain | 35.5% |
| Schedule | 18.6% |
| Congestion | 14.4% |
| Weather METAR | Low |
| Weather TAF | Low |

::: {.callout-tip}
## Key Finding

`dep_hour` is the single most important feature (#1 individual feature). Under strict leakage control, schedule-derived information systematically dominates prediction.
:::

## Schedule Dominance {#schedule}

::: {.callout-tip}
## Core Finding: The Schedule Tells You If a Flight Will Be Delayed

More than half of the predictable component of delays is already determined the moment the schedule is published.
:::

**Plain Language:** Delays are not random accidents but systematic structural problems. Departure time, aircraft type, route traffic — these factors, already determined when the schedule is published, dominate delay predictability.

**Comparison with Literature:**

- Existing literature considers weather a major factor
- However, most of these studies did not strictly control for leakage
- Under our strict conditions, schedule is far more important than weather

**Novelty of This Finding:**

- One paper (FDPP-ML, 2023, *Journal of Big Data*) stated "no current study can predict delays from schedule features alone" and then did so themselves
- But no paper has used SHAP to quantify and explain "what proportion does schedule dominate, and why"
- Rigorously validated on 19.15M flights nationwide, not a geographic anomaly

## Weather Near-Zero Contribution {#weather}

::: {.callout-tip}
## Core Finding

Under strict pre-departure leakage control, weather features' SHAP contribution approaches zero.
:::

**Why:**

- Weather observations (METAR) 2-6 hours before departure represent **current weather**, not weather at arrival time
- Destination weather forecasts (TAF) have large prediction errors for long-haul flights
- Weather appearing "useful" in other papers is likely due to using post-departure or post-arrival weather observations (leakage)

**Comparison with Literature:**

- Hatipoglu & Tosun (2024, MDPI): Also found weather unimportant, but attributed it to Turkey's stable climate
- Alfarhood et al. (2024, Wiley): Weather unimportant in Saudi Arabia, attributed to desert climate
- **Our finding differs**: U.S. weather is complex, yet weather still near-zero → the reason is **methodological**, not geographic

::: {.callout-warning}
## Implication for Literature

Most existing literature's weather feature importance may be overestimated due to leakage.
:::

:::

```{=html}
<script>
window.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {
  var hash = window.location.hash.replace('#', '');
  if (!hash) return;
  var tabs = document.querySelectorAll('[data-bs-toggle="tab"], [data-bs-toggle="pill"]');
  for (var i = 0; i < tabs.length; i++) {
    var target = tabs[i].getAttribute('data-bs-target') || tabs[i].getAttribute('href') || '';
    if (target.indexOf(hash) !== -1) {
      tabs[i].click();
      break;
    }
  }
});
</script>
```
